purpose: Pin the offline replay fixture so the bridge feasibility check is reproducible, self-reference-free (DE-0132), and rubric-free. Feasibility of wiring only — NOT an HBB-30 correctness/REC2 result.

hbb30_subset_canonical.def: json.dumps([r for r in records if r.incident=='HBB-30'], sort_keys=True, ensure_ascii=False, separators=(',',':'))

question_context.note: neutral stuck-frame; NOT answer-aware; no rubric

corpus_rule: the answer_question corpus for this replay is EXCLUSIVELY the adapted HBB-30 intermediate records. EGL's own ledgers (DESIGN_EVIDENCE_LEDGER, REVIEW_LEDGER, audit_backlog) are NOT fed (no self-reference, DE-0132).
retrieval_rule: k = number of fed records (>= n_candidates) so no candidate is silently dropped; if any truncation occurs it MUST be logged (PROCESS-01: no silent caps).

decoding.determinism_note: answer_question is temp=0 but the run records raw output; feasibility does not assert bitwise determinism

forbidden_in_replay: feeding EGL ledgers into the corpus
expected_form: answer_dict with ANSWER_KEYS; answer_claims non-empty (the material supports a candidate) AND open_gaps non-empty (unresolved considerations present in the material); validate_answer M1 source_trace completeness > 0.
success_definition: WIRING feasibility = (a) adapter produced grounded records, (b) answer_question returned a valid ANSWER_KEYS object, (c) answer_claims and open_gaps both populated and coexisting, (d) validate_answer passed M1/M3. NOT a claim that the answer is correct or that HBB-30 is solved.
claim_ceiling: mechanism-feasibility only. No HBB-30 solved. No REC2. No Attention-Center. No structural re-centering. C != H.
